reasoning: There is currently no licensed vaccine for Ureaplasma urealyticum. Recent research from June 2025 involves reverse vaccinology (computational identification of epitopes), and a 2022 paper identifies a candidate protein, but these are at the preclinical or in silico stages. Vaccine development, clinical trials, and regulatory approval consistently take many years. Therefore, it is virtually impossible that a vaccine will be fully developed, approved, and recognized on Wikipedia by early 2026.
